decision: Preserve active Greek Esther named-addition form, add explicit translation-object lanes, and preserve source-address rows where bound ordinary sources have rows absent from active NRSV.

activeNRSVPolicy.rule: Existing active strings may be converted to text.NRSV objects without changing the NRSV text, but active NRSV Greek Esther is not source-trusted until an approved NRSV/NRSVA source witness is bound and collated.
rowsAbsentFromNRSV: Esther 8:13-17 must remain absent from NRSV unless an approved NRSV source witness provides those addresses.

ordinaryChapterPolicy.KJV: Populate KJV for ordinary Esther 1-10 from bound KJV 1611 Esther source. Insert active Esther 8:13-17 as KJV source-address rows because the active NRSV grid currently stops at 8:12.
ordinaryChapterPolicy.NABRE: Populate NABRE for ordinary Esther 1-10 from bound historical NABRE Esther source. Insert active Esther 8:13-17 as NABRE source-address rows because the active NRSV grid currently stops at 8:12.
ordinaryChapterPolicy.DRB: Populate DRB ordinary Esther source-address rows 1-10, with chapter 8:13-17 inserted and chapter 10:4-13 mapped only through Addition F policy rather than ordinary chapter 10 display.

Addition D.reason: Active Addition D begins with Esther's third-day approach to the king; DRB 15:4 begins that same scene, while DRB 15:1-3 are a preceding Mordecai/transition block.

sourceExtraPolicy.reason: The active named Addition D grid starts at the third-day approach scene. These preceding rows are not present in active NRSV Addition D and are not inserted without a separate display/address policy.

nextRequiredWork: Add bounded lane-expansion repair for KJV/NABRE ordinary rows and DRB ordinary/addition rows under this policy.
Run exact source collation for KJV/NABRE ordinary lanes and DRB ordinary/addition lanes.
Keep NRSV Greek Esther blocked pending approved source witness.
